title: LongCat: Efficient Long-Context Attention for Language Models
preview: Introduces LongCat attention approximation enabling 1M+ token context windows with near-linear compute and memory scaling.
summary: LongCat proposes a hierarchical chunked attention mechanism that decomposes long sequences into local and global attention components, reducing attention memory from O(n²) to near-O(n). Achieves quality comparable to full attention on long-document benchmarks while enabling practical inference at million-token context lengths.
datacenter: High relevance to long-context serving infrastructure. LongCat-style approaches determine whether next-generation long-context LLMs can run on today's GPU memory configurations or require new hardware with higher DRAM capacity per device.
metrics: Key result signal: memory scaling factor and quality retention at 256K–1M token contexts versus full attention.
